title: Origins in Victorian England
description: Table tennis originated as an after-dinner parlor game among upper-class Victorians in England, using improvised equipment like cigar box lids and champagne corks.

title: First Official Tournament
description: The first official table tennis tournament was held in England, attracting over 300 players and marking the sport's transition from parlor game to competitive activity.

title: ITTF Founded
description: The International Table Tennis Federation (ITTF) was established in Berlin, unifying rules and organizing the first World Championships.

title: Asian Dominance Begins
description: Japan revolutionized the sport with the penhold grip and sponge rubber, ushering in an era of Asian dominance that continues today.

title: Ping Pong Diplomacy
description: The historic exchange between American and Chinese table tennis players helped thaw Cold War relations and paved the way for Nixon's visit to China.

title: Olympic Debut
description: Table tennis became an official Olympic sport at the Seoul Games, elevating its global profile and competitive prestige.

title: Modern Era
description: Introduction of the 40mm ball, 11-point scoring system, and advanced rubbers transformed the game into a faster, more spectator-friendly sport.

title: Technology & Global Growth
description: Video replay systems, robot training partners, and growing popularity across all continents mark the latest chapter in table tennis evolution.
